_meta.name: English
_meta.comment: English translations for CineSort v7.7.0. Glossary (30 terms) + FAQ (15 questions) translated from web/dashboard/views/help.js + docs/MANUAL.md. Keys MUST mirror fr.json. Interpolation: t('key', { var: 'value' }) -> '{{var}}' is replaced. Technical names preserved (TMDb, Jellyfin, LPIPS, EBU R128, etc.).

fields.composite_score_hint: V1 by default. V2 uses enriched weights and adjustment rules. Existing scores will NOT be re-computed automatically.
fields.tmdb_cache_ttl_hint: Beyond this, entries are refreshed. Auto-purge at startup.

preview_effects.title: Visual effects preview
preview_effects.hint: Move the sliders above to see the effect in real time on this preview.

qr.rest_disabled: Enable "REST API" above to generate a QR code and an access URL.
qr.no_token: No access key. Regenerate one with the 🔄 button above.
qr.scan_hint: Scan the QR code or open the URL on any device on your local Wi-Fi network.

expert_mode.hint: Disabled by default. Enable to tweak timeouts, ports, retries, HTTPS, SMTP, etc.

settings.rest_token_regen_confirm:
Generate a new access key?

All connected devices will need to reconnect.
settings.rest_token_regen_success: ✓ New key. Auto-saving, then restart the service.

quality.subtitle: V2 distribution, 30d trend, grouping by dimension. Analyze, simulate, export your profile.
quality.no_data_hint: Run a scan + analysis to see the perceptual score of your movies.
quality.outliers_hint: Movies more than 2σ below mean — click for details
quality.chart_no_quality: No quality analysis. Run a scan.
quality.chart_no_trend: Several runs needed for the trend.
quality.outlier_modal_body:
Row ID: {{rid}}

Open the Library for the full inspector of the movie.

integrations.subtitle: External connections (Jellyfin, Plex, Radarr, TMDb). Test connection + check consistency + libraries.
integrations.sync_summary_radarr: {{matched}} matched — {{notInRadarr}} missing in Radarr — {{upgrade}} upgrade candidates

journal.no_active_run: No run in progress. Start a scan from the Processing tab to see live logs.

danger_zone.title: Danger zone
danger_zone.reset_desc: Deletes your database, settings, run history, TMDb caches and perceptual analyses.
danger_zone.reset_preserve: Preserves: your video files (never touched), the logs (useful for debugging).
danger_zone.reset_backup: An automatic ZIP backup is created before deletion in the parent folder.
danger_zone.prompt_confirm:
You are about to delete {{items}} files ({{size}} MB).

Type "RESET" to confirm:
danger_zone.last_chance:
LAST CHANCE: continue the reset?
(a backup will be created before deletion)

sidebar.integration_disabled_title: {{label}} not configured — click to enable it in Settings

glossary.tier: Final quality tier of a movie. 5 levels: Platinum (excellence) > Gold (very good) > Silver (decent) > Bronze (limited) > Reject (needs redo). Combines composite score V2 + contextual adjustments (era, codec, perceptual).
glossary.perceptual_score: Visual quality score computed via image analysis (luminance, grain, banding). Part of the perceptual engine. Combined with the audio perceptual score into the composite Score V2.
glossary.score_v2: Composite Score V2 — composite rating out of 100 (CinemaLux v2). Weights Video 60%, Audio 35%, Coherence 5%. Includes perceptual analysis if enabled. More readable than legacy V1 (kept for compatibility).
glossary.score_v1: Composite Score V1 — legacy scoring system (pre-v7.5.0). Based only on metadata (resolution, codec, audio). Kept for reports created before migration 011.
glossary.confidence: Certainty level of the TMDb match. 0.95+ = certain (.nfo + IMDb id). 0.7-0.95 = strong. 0.5-0.7 = medium (review). <0.5 = weak (manual check needed).
glossary.probe: Action of analyzing a video file to extract its technical characteristics (codec, resolution, bitrate, audio tracks, HDR). Tools used: ffprobe or mediainfo, in parallel.
glossary.run: Run — a complete scan execution (unique identifier, dedicated journal). Each scan creates a new run with its own logs and reports.
glossary.plan: Plan — list of proposed changes (renames + moves) generated at the end of a scan. Reviewable before apply.
glossary.apply: Apply — execute the plan for real (modifies the disk). Creates an entry in the apply journal for later undo.
glossary.undo: Undo — cancellation of an apply operation. Can be done per batch (full rollback) or per movie (Undo v5). Crash-safe via write-ahead journal.
glossary.quarantine: Quarantine — the `_review/` folder where invalid files are moved: movies without TMDb match, SHA1 duplicates (`_duplicates_identical/`), conflicts (`_review/_conflicts`), corrupted files. You can review and rescue them manually.
glossary.dry_run: Dry-run — preview mode: CineSort computes all changes (moves, renames) but does not touch any file. You see the full report before applying for real. Recommended for the first pass.
glossary.nfo: NFO — XML metadata file (Kodi/Jellyfin/Emby format) placed next to the video (title, year, IMDb/TMDb id). CineSort reads it as a priority source (more reliable than the filename) and can also generate it.
glossary.tmdb: TMDb (The Movie Database) — free collaborative database providing title, year, poster, collection. Used as the canonical source for movie metadata.
glossary.jellyfin: Jellyfin — open-source media server. CineSort can trigger Jellyfin library refresh after each apply, and synchronize watched/unwatched status.
glossary.plex: Plex — proprietary media server. CineSort connects via X-Plex-Token to refresh the Plex library after an apply.
glossary.radarr: Radarr — automated movie collection manager. CineSort can propose quality upgrades for monitored movies via the Radarr API.
glossary.quality_profile: Quality profile — set of weights and thresholds for the scoring system (CinemaLux). Tells CineSort what matters most (resolution, codec, audio, subtitles).
glossary.naming_preset: Naming preset — predefined renaming template. 5 presets bundled: default, plex, jellyfin, quality, custom. Variables like {title}, {year}, {resolution} are filled at apply time.
glossary.multi_root: Multi-root — ability to scan several root folders (SSD + NAS + external disk) in a single run. Each root has its own buckets (`_review`, `_Collection`, `_Empty`). Cross-root duplicates are detected.
glossary.watch_folder: Watch folder (auto-monitor) — surveillance mode: CineSort polls the root every N minutes (1-60) and triggers a scan automatically when new files are detected. No external dependency, pure Python threading.
glossary.plugin_hook: Plugin hook — external script (Python, .bat, .ps1) automatically triggered after an event (post_scan, post_apply, post_undo, post_error). Receives JSON context via stdin and CINESORT_EVENT/CINESORT_RUN_ID env vars.
glossary.smart_playlist: Smart playlist — saved filter inside the library view (e.g. "all Nolan films in 4K HDR"). Persisted across sessions with full CRUD operations.
glossary.composite_score_v2: Composite Score V2 — second generation scoring engine. Combines visual (60%), audio (35%) and coherence (5%) categories, with 9 contextual adjustment rules and confidence-weighted scoring. Coexists with V1 via setting `composite_score_version`.
glossary.banding: Banding — visual artifact: visible bands in skies and gradients. Caused by overly aggressive compression. Detected by the perceptual engine via block variance analysis.
glossary.upscale: Upscale (suspect) — file advertised as 4K but with a bitrate too low for its resolution. Likely a 1080p source stretched to 4K. Flagged by the encode analysis module.
glossary.reencode: Re-encode (degraded) — file re-encoded with too low a bitrate, causing visible quality loss. Detected via codec/bitrate heuristics in `encode_analysis.py`.
glossary.hdr10: HDR10 — base High Dynamic Range standard, static metadata (single peak luminance for the whole movie). Universal compatibility.
glossary.dolby_vision: Dolby Vision (DV) — premium HDR standard with dynamic per-shot metadata. Profiles 5/7/8.1/8.2/8.4 detected via DOVI record. More precise than HDR10 but requires compatible playback chain.
glossary.grain_v2: Grain analysis v2 — perceptual classification of film grain by era (16mm 1920+, 35mm classic 1950+, 35mm modern 1990+, digital noise 2000+, digital clean 2010+, UHD/Dolby Vision 2015+). Detects authentic grain vs. excessive DNR (smoothing). Includes director-specific exceptions (Nolan, A24, Pixar).
glossary.lpips: LPIPS (Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity) — scientific measure of visual similarity using a pre-trained AlexNet ML model. More reliable than SSIM or PSNR for perceived quality. Used to compare duplicates.
glossary.ebu_r128: EBU R128 (loudness) — European Broadcasting Union audio loudness standard. Measures integrated loudness (LUFS), loudness range (LRA) and true peak. Detects loudness wars and broadcast normalization.
glossary.saga_collection: Saga / Collection (TMDb) — grouping of related movies (e.g. The Lord of the Rings, Marvel Cinematic Universe). CineSort detects collections via TMDb and can create a parent `_Collection/` folder to keep them together.
glossary.edition: Edition (Director's Cut, Extended, IMAX, etc.) — particular version of a movie. CineSort detects it from the filename or .nfo and keeps it in the final folder title. Edition-aware deduplication: same movie + different editions ≠ duplicates.
glossary.ssim: SSIM (Structural Similarity Index) — compares two images on structure + luminance + contrast. Used to detect fake 4K (an upscale has high SSIM with its 1080p downscaled version).
glossary.chromaprint: Chromaprint — audio fingerprint generated by fpcalc. Detects that two versions of a movie (FR + EN) share the same soundtrack without comparing the full files.
glossary.drc: DRC (Dynamic Range Compression) — audio compression level. Cinema = wide dynamic range preserved (loud music, quiet dialogue). Standard = slightly compressed. Broadcast = heavily compressed (TV, more uniform but flattened).
glossary.perceptual: Perceptual analysis — analyzes the actual image and audio quality (not just metadata). Detects fake 4K (upscale), excessive DNR, fake HDR, mono audio disguised as 5.1, etc. Enabled in Settings > Perceptual.

help.first_run: First launch? Start the wizard from Settings > Start wizard.
help.support_intro: Couldn't find your answer? Several channels are available:
help.report_bug: Report a bug or ask a question
help.diagnostic: Exportable diagnostic: Settings > Logs > Export diagnostic. API keys and SMTP passwords are automatically scrubbed from the zip.
help.shortcuts_intro: Memorize these combinations to save time. Alt+digit shortcuts work even inside text fields; bare digits only work outside a field.

q_first_scan.question: How do I run my first scan?
q_first_scan.answer: Go to Settings > Root folders, add the folder containing your movies (e.g. D:\Movies), save, then open Library > Scan. CineSort reads each video file, fetches info from TMDb and generates a full analysis. On first launch, the 5-step wizard guides you automatically.

q_dry_run.question: What is a dry-run?
q_dry_run.answer: A dry-run simulates applying changes without moving or renaming anything on your disk. You see exactly what would be done. It's the recommended option for your first pass: zero risk of losing a file. Uncheck "Dry-run mode" in the Apply screen only when you are confident.

q_auto_approve.question: How does auto-approval work?
q_auto_approve.answer: When a movie has a high score and strong confidence (exact TMDb match + consistent .nfo), CineSort marks it as approved automatically. You can manually uncheck the ones you don't want to apply. Setting: Settings > Validation > confidence thresholds.

q_decisions_lost.question: My decisions disappeared after refresh, why?
q_decisions_lost.answer: Validation decisions are persisted to the SQLite database via Ctrl+S or the Save button. If you didn't save before an F5 or a view change, they are lost. CineSort displays a "unsaved decisions" warning if you navigate without saving.

q_undo_apply.question: How do I undo an apply?
q_undo_apply.answer: CineSort keeps a journal of every move/rename (apply_operations table). Go to Apply > Undo, choose the batch to revert, preview as a dry-run, then confirm. Undo can be per full batch or per movie (Undo v5). Conflicts (file modified since) are redirected to `_review/_undo_conflicts`.

q_configure_integrations.question: How do I configure Jellyfin / Plex / Radarr?
q_configure_integrations.answer: Settings > Integrations section. For Jellyfin: server URL + API key (Profile > API Keys in Jellyfin). For Plex: URL + X-Plex-Token (retrievable via plex.tv/account). For Radarr: URL + API key (Settings > General). The "Test connection" button validates immediately. Enable auto-refresh to trigger a scan after each apply.

q_scan_no_movies.question: My scan finds no movies, what should I do?
q_scan_no_movies.answer: Check in this order: (1) is the root folder accessible (no disconnected NAS); (2) are the video extensions supported (mkv, mp4, avi, mov by default); (3) are the files read-only or blocked by antivirus; (4) check the Logs view for the exact error message. If nothing works, export the logs and report via GitHub Issues.

q_perceptual_mode.question: What is perceptual mode?
q_perceptual_mode.answer: Perceptual analysis looks at the actual image and audio quality (not just metadata). It detects fake 4K (upscale), excessive DNR (smoothing that erases grain), audio DRC (cinema/standard/broadcast compression), badly encoded HDR, etc. Setting: Settings > Perceptual. Enable if you want to grade the real quality, not just the displayed resolution.

q_install_ffmpeg.question: How do I install ffmpeg / mediainfo?
q_install_ffmpeg.answer: Settings > Video tools > "Install automatically" button. CineSort downloads the official binaries and places them in the app folder, without touching the system PATH. If you prefer a manual install: ffmpeg.org and mediaarea.net/MediaInfo, then provide the full path in Settings.

q_share_logs.question: How do I share my logs to report a bug?
q_share_logs.answer: Settings > Logs > "Export diagnostic" button. The zip file contains logs + version + config (without your API keys, automatically scrubbed: TMDb, Jellyfin, Plex, Radarr, SMTP passwords). Attach it to a GitHub issue. Raw logs: %LOCALAPPDATA%/CineSort/logs/cinesort.log (rotation 50 MB x 5).

q_install_antivirus.question: My antivirus says CineSort is dangerous
q_install_antivirus.answer: Common false positive for Python EXEs compiled with PyInstaller. CineSort is open source (readable code on GitHub) and signed. Solutions: (1) add an exception for CineSort.exe in your antivirus; (2) verify the binary signature (right-click > Properties > Digital signatures); (3) compile from source if you prefer. No telemetry, no network code outside TMDb/Jellyfin/Plex/Radarr (which you enable yourself).

q_dashboard_connection.question: The remote dashboard won't connect
q_dashboard_connection.answer: Check: (1) Settings > REST API is enabled; (2) you are using the full access token (at least 32 characters for LAN binding); (3) Windows firewall allows CineSort.exe on port 8642; (4) PC and phone are on the same Wi-Fi. The "Remote access" card on the Home page shows the URL and a QR code to scan.

q_tier_meaning.question: What do the tiers Platinum / Gold / Silver / Bronze / Reject mean?
q_tier_meaning.answer: It's the final movie quality grade across 5 levels. Platinum = excellence (4K HDR Dolby Atmos with no flaws). Gold = very good version (clean 1080p or 4K). Silver = decent (DVD or 720p). Bronze = limited (low resolution or degrading encode). Reject = needs redo (fake 4K, mono audio, corrupted file). The calculation combines resolution, codec, audio, subtitles and perceptual detection.

q_export_report.question: How do I export a report of my analysis?
q_export_report.answer: After a scan: Library > Apply > "Export" button. Three formats: HTML (standalone report with charts, openable in any browser, printable as PDF via Ctrl+P); enriched CSV (30 columns, openable in Excel); .nfo XML (Kodi/Jellyfin/Emby, one file per movie next to the video).

q_offline_mode.question: Can I use CineSort without an Internet connection?
q_offline_mode.answer: Yes for local scanning and applying. No for TMDb enrichment (original title, year, poster, collection). Tip: run a first scan online to fill the local TMDb cache, then you can work offline. Subtitles are detected locally without Internet. Jellyfin/Plex/Radarr work as long as your server is reachable (LAN is enough).
